figures.caption: Table 1. Quantitative comparison of baselines. ↑ and ↓ indicate that higher and lower values are better, respectively. Bold, underline, and italic represent the first-, second-, and third-best results, respectively.
Fig. 4. Qualitative evaluation. We present stylized motion sequences generated with three different style reference and context description pairs. Brighter colors indicate earlier frames.

Table 3. Ablation studies on supervised contrastive loss and style encoder guidance across four variants of our model with different training data distributions. The first column indicates the number of styles used for training.

Fig. 5. Ablation study results on the supervised contrastive loss and the style encoder guidance when trained on 25 styles. (a)–(d) annotations follow those in Tab. 3: (a) full, (b) without Lsupcon, (c) without style encoder guidance, and (d) without both. Brighter colors indicate earlier frames.
Fig. 7. Comparison of motions generated using models trained on 100 styles and 25 styles. Reference styles are unseen in the 25-style setting. Brighter colors indicate earlier frames.
Fig. 2. Method overview. Our method conditions motion generation on a text prompt for content and a reference motion sequence for style, producing motions that jointly reflect both content and style.
